specific_location: Southern fenceline of Lone Pine pasture on Meadow Springs Ranch.  40.97231N, 104.92138W
description: Sparrow shaped but a bit larger, with bright yellow breast. Male had distinct black throat patch and rufous colored shoulders, light gray belly. Female had yellow breast, light gray belly and rufous shoulders. No black throat patch.Behaviors: Male was singing from nearby fencepost, female was bringing food back to hidden nestling in dense, short shrub. Female was very cautious, and called for ~20min in shrub where nestling was hiding. Got a good long look at her while perched approximately 15 feet away, as well as male perched approximately 40 feet away. Both were trying to distract me from area and nestling.Call: Male song: "see,see,dick,dick,dick" Female call: short "pwik"Plumage: Breeding
